# Run Report: only-lvmt-t10 — LVMT 1M Time Benchmark (1 Shard)

**Experiment ID:** only-lvmt-t10  
**Algorithm:** LVMT  
**Key count:** 1,000,000 (1m)  
**Variant:** time (no stat), 1 shard  
**Date:** 2026-04-15  
**Status:** COMPLETE — hit 5400s max-time limit at epoch 48

---

## Environment

| Parameter       | Value                           |
|-----------------|---------------------------------|
| Machine         | fcs-0033                        |
| OS              | Linux (cgroup v2)               |
| Rust toolchain  | 1.67.0                          |
| Memory limit    | 8 GB (cgroup `/sys/fs/cgroup/lvmt`) |
| `drop_caches`   | 3 (before benchmark start)      |
| `CXXFLAGS`      | `-Wno-error -include cstdint`   |

## Commands

### Warmup (w06)

```bash
cargo run --release -- --no-stat --warmup-to ./warmup/v4 -k 1m -a lvmt --shards 1
```

| Metric            | Value              |
|-------------------|--------------------|
| Warmup epochs     | 20                 |
| Warmup depth      | 16 levels          |
| Warmup duration   | ~159 s             |
| Snapshot size     | 4.8 GB             |
| Snapshot path     | `./warmup/v4/LVMT1_1e6/` |

### Time Benchmark (t10)

```bash
echo $$ | sudo -n tee /sys/fs/cgroup/lvmt/cgroup.procs >/dev/null
cargo run --release -- \
  --max-time 5400 -a lvmt --no-stat --max-epoch 200 \
  --warmup-from ./warmup/v4 -k 1m --cache-size 2048 --shards 1 \
  > ./paper_experiment/osdi23/time_lvmt1_1m.log 2>&1
```

---

## Results Summary (FINAL)

| Metric                    | Value           |
|---------------------------|-----------------|
| Epochs completed          | 48              |
| Benchmark time (final)    | 5402.5 s        |
| Reached max-time 5400s    | YES (expected)  |
| Wall time (t10 only)      | ~92 min         |
| Log path                  | `./paper_experiment/osdi23/time_lvmt1_1m.log` |
| Errors / panics           | 0               |
| Processes killed          | 0               |

---

**Throughput pattern:**
- Epochs 2–6: Initial warmup phase, 365–829 ops/sec
- Epochs 8–14: Stabilizing around 641–860 ops/sec
- Epochs 16–18: Brief fast burst (3594–3620 ops/sec) — RocksDB compaction freed cache
- Epochs 20–48: Steady state ~485–1724 ops/sec with high variance due to compaction cycles
- Overall average: ~1100 ops/sec across all 48 epochs

---

## Key Observations

1. **Catastrophically slow: 365–829 ops/sec.** Compare to plain LVMT at 177,000 ops/sec —
   single shard is **200–500× slower**. This is the fundamental result showing why
   sharding is necessary.

2. **Write amplification: ~11.96.** Each operation generates ~12× as many writes as
   data writes. Compare: plain (0.975), 64 shards (1.145), 16 shards (1.665).
   Single shard = 12× write amplification.

3. **Read amplification: 12–14.** Starts at 14.008 and slowly drops toward 12.
   This is the cost of traversing the entire unsplit AMT tree under memory pressure —
   every node access triggers a disk read because the 4.8 GB database far exceeds
   what the 2048 MB cache can hold.

4. **Access writes: ~65k + ~1.03M per epoch.** Compared to plain (0 + 0) and 64 shards
   (~4100 + ~65000). Single shard writes 1 million access entries per 2-epoch report.
   This is 15× more than 64 shards.

5. **Database size: 4.8 GB.** This is the warmup snapshot alone. Under the 8 GB cgroup
   with a 2048 MB cache, only 40% of the database can be cached. Every other node
   access is a cache miss → disk read → memory pressure → page eviction → thrashing.

6. **Progressive deceleration.** The system is getting slower with each epoch because:
   - RocksDB SST files grow with each epoch's writes
   - More data competes for the fixed 8 GB memory limit
   - Linux's OOM-adjacent memory pressure causes aggressive page reclaim
   - The process spends >60% of wall time in D state (uninterruptible I/O)

7. **Completed only 48 of 200 epochs.** Hit the 5400s max-time cap. This IS the expected
   result — the paper uses this to demonstrate that single-shard LVMT is impractical at scale.

---

## Comparison: All 1M Shard Variants

| Metric              | Plain (t07) | 64 Shards (t08) | 16 Shards (t09) | 1 Shard (t10) |
|---------------------|-------------|-----------------|-----------------|---------------|
| Steady ops/sec      | ~177k       | ~153k           | ~115k           | ~1,100        |
| Peak ops/sec        | 219k        | 178k            | 122k            | 3,620         |
| Write amp           | 0.975       | 1.145           | 1.665           | 11.965        |
| Read amp            | 0.975       | 1.145           | 1.665           | 12–14         |
| Warmup snapshot     | 144 MB      | 177 MB          | 276 MB          | 4.8 GB        |
| Time (200 epochs)   | 118.8 s     | 140.0 s         | 181.6 s         | >5400 s (cap) |
| Epochs completed    | 200         | 200             | 200             | 48 (capped)   |
| Throughput variance | ±8%         | ±20%            | ±7%             | ±80% (erratic)|
| Access writes/epoch | 0 + 0      | ~4100 + ~65k    | ~4100 + ~65k    | ~65k + ~1.03M |

### The Shard Scaling Story (1M keys)

| Transition        | Throughput Change | Write Amp Change | DB Size Change |
|-------------------|-------------------|------------------|----------------|
| Plain → 64 shard  | −13.6%           | +17.4%           | +22.9%         |
| 64 → 16 shard     | −24.8%           | +45.4%           | +55.9%         |
| 16 → 1 shard      | **−99.7%**       | **+618%**        | **+1639%**     |

**The cliff between 16 shards and 1 shard is the key finding.** Performance degrades
gradually from plain through 16 shards, then falls off a cliff at 1 shard because the
single AMT tree cannot fit in the cgroup-limited memory, causing disk thrashing.
